reason: The statement applies because the text describes a series of events where the LORD demonstrates his power by sending various plagues upon Egypt, including turning the water to blood, sending frogs, lice, flies, pestilence, boils, hail, locusts, darkness, and the death of the firstborn. Each plague highlights the power of the LORD over the forces of nature and the gods of Egypt, emphasizing the stark contrast between light (symbolizing life, prosperity, and the favor of the LORD) and darkness (symbolizing destruction, death, and judgment).
